name: render-info
description: Using the RendererInfo class in Blazor to detect rendering context and interactivity.
capabilities: Detect if component is interactive using RendererInfo.IsInteractive
Identify render mode name (Static, Server, WebAssembly, WebView)
Conditionally render static vs interactive content
Use AssignedRenderMode for component-level render mode detection
author: AndreHogberg
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown content about Blazor's RendererInfo class. All 49 static findings are false positives: the scanner misidentified C# Razor syntax (@if, @onclick) as shell commands, triggered on common words in JSON metadata as crypto algorithms, and flagged the source_url field as a network threat. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or filesystem access exists.
valueStatement: Blazor components need different behavior during static rendering versus interactive modes. This skill provides documentation and code examples for using RendererInfo to detect rendering context, disable inputs during prerendering, and conditionally render content based on interactivity.
userTitle: Detect Blazor render modes with RendererInfo
limitations: RendererInfo is available in .NET 8.0 and later only
AssignedRenderMode may be null during static rendering
Cannot change render mode at runtime once component is initialized
Documentation only - no code execution or file generation

useCases.title: Fix prerendering bugs
useCases.description: Disable buttons and inputs during server prerendering to prevent user confusion when event handlers are not yet active.

useCases.title: Adaptive UI patterns
useCases.description: Show simplified HTML forms for static SSR and rich interactive components for WebAssembly or Server modes.

useCases.title: Debug render issues
useCases.description: Display current render mode in application for troubleshooting unexpected behavior across different hosting models.

promptTemplates.title: Basic usage
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use RendererInfo.IsInteractive in Blazor to check if my component is running interactively?

promptTemplates.title: Render mode detection
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the possible values for RendererInfo.Name and what do they mean in Blazor?

promptTemplates.prompt: How do I disable a button during Blazor prerendering so users cannot click it before JavaScript loads?

promptTemplates.title: Content switching
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to render a simple HTML form for static SSR and a Blazor component for interactive modes using RendererInfo.

bestPractices: Check RendererInfo.IsInteractive before attaching event handlers to prevent errors during prerendering
Use RendererInfo.Name for logging and debugging to understand which renderer is active
Combine RendererInfo with ComponentBase.AssignedRenderMode for complete render mode awareness
antiPatterns: Assuming event handlers work during prerendering without checking RendererInfo.IsInteractive
Using hardcoded renderer names instead of the built-in constants or detection methods
Relying solely on AssignedRenderMode during prerendering since it may be null

faq.question: What .NET version supports RendererInfo?
faq.answer: RendererInfo is available in .NET 8.0 and later versions only.

faq.question: What are the possible RendererInfo.Name values?
faq.answer: Static, Server, WebAssembly, and WebView depending on the hosting model.

faq.question: How does RendererInfo differ from AssignedRenderMode?
faq.answer: RendererInfo shows current runtime state; AssignedRenderMode shows what was assigned to the component.

faq.question: Is data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, this is a documentation-only skill with no file access or network calls.

faq.question: Why are buttons sometimes disabled in my Blazor app?
faq.answer: Buttons may be disabled during prerendering when event handlers are not yet active. Use RendererInfo.IsInteractive to control this.

faq.question: Can I use this with Blazor Hybrid apps?
faq.answer: Yes, RendererInfo.Name returns WebView for Blazor Hybrid applications running in MAUI, WPF, or WinForms.
